summary: French composer (1838–1875), best known for the opera Carmen (1875). Also composed The Pearl Fishers (Les pêcheurs de perles, 1863), the incidental music for Alphonse Daudet's L'Arlésienne (arranged into two suites, both concluding with the 'March of the Kings' theme), and a precocious Symphony in C (written at 17, first performed 60 years after his death). His teacher was Charles Gounod. Ernest Guiraud arranged the second L'Arlésienne suite and wrote recitatives for Carmen.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: French composer (1838–1875). His teacher at the Paris Conservatory was Charles Gounod. He suppressed his Symphony in C because of its similarities with Gounod's Symphony in D. He also completed Fromental Halévy's unfinished opera Noé. Ernest Guiraud arranged the second L'Arlésienne suite and wrote orchestral recitatives for Carmen and The Tales of Hoffmann. Bizet set words by Charles Grandmougin to music. He died in 1875, the same year Carmen premiered. Wikipedia

works.name: Carmen
works.description: Bizet's final and most famous opera (1875), set in Seville. The title character sings the Habanera comparing love to a 'rebellious bird'; Don José is seduced and ultimately murders Carmen outside the bullring while Escamillo sings the Toreador Song. Based on a Prosper Mérimée novella. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Carmen sings the Habanera ('L'amour est un oiseau rebelle'), comparing love to a 'rebellious bird'; Escamillo sings the Toreador Song
examples: his most famous work features a dancer who seduces the soldier Don Jose, listens to Escamillo sing the Toreador Song, and sings the Habanera
The title character of one of his operas sings a habanera, and flirts with Escamillo the toreador and with Don Jose.

clues.clue: The Habanera melody was unknowingly taken from a Sebastián Iradier song (believed to be a folk tune); Carmen warns 'if I love you, best beware'
examples: Bizet unknowingly used a melody written by Sebastian Yradier as the basis for this aria.
an aria whose repeated downward chromatic motions contrast with a lyric claiming love 'has never, ever, known a law'

clues.clue: Based on Prosper Mérimée's novella Carmen
examples: This composer wrote an opera based on Prosper Merimee's novel Carmen.
a soprano role that was not present in the original novella by Prosper Merimee

clues.clue: Micaëla's Act III aria is 'Je dis que rien ne m'épouvante'; she was added to the opera and not in the original Mérimée novella
examples: This character prays to God to protect her in her Act 3 aria 'Je dis que rien ne m'epouvante.' [She] was not present in the original novella by Prosper Merimee. As an innocent village maiden, she serves as a foil to the brash and indecent title character.

clues.clue: In Act II a group of women sing the tavern song 'Les tringles des sistres tintaient'; Carmen sings 'En vain pour éviter' (the card aria)
examples: In the second act of this opera, a group of women sing the tavern song 'Les tringles des sistres tintaient.'
the title character sings 'En vain pour eviter,' and a village girl expresses her determination to save the protagonist in 'Je dis que rien ne m'epouvante'

works.name: The Pearl Fishers (Les pêcheurs de perles)
works.description: Bizet's 1863 opera set in ancient Sri Lanka (Ceylon). Two men — the baritone Zurga and the tenor Nadir — are lifelong friends who discover they are both in love with the Brahman priestess Leila. They sing the famous friendship duet 'Au fond du temple saint' in Act I. Hector Berlioz gave it a glowing review in his last feuilleton. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Zurga and Nadir sing the friendship duet 'Au fond du temple saint'; both are in love with Leila, a priestess of Brahma
examples: Nadir and Zurga sing the friendship duet 'Au fond du temple saint' in spite of their mutual love for the Ceylonese princess Leila.
Two friends sing about falling in love with a Brahman priestess at the same moment in his duet, 'Au fond du temple saint'.

clues.clue: Set in Sri Lanka (Ceylon); Nadir and Zurga are pearl fishers; Leila is a priestess of Brahma; high priest of Brahma is named Nourabad
examples: This opera set in Sri Lanka includes the tenor and baritone duet 'Au fond du temple saint.'
This opera set in Ceylon in which Zurga and Nadir vie for the love of the Brahman priestess Leila.

clues.clue: Leila's aria 'Comme autrefois dans la nuit sombre' recalls happier times; she also sings 'Je le jure' (three-part oath of chastity)
examples: This composer wrote the aria 'Comme autrefois,' in which Leila recalls happier times, for an opera whose characters include a high priest of Brahma named Nourabad.
Another of this composer's characters calls on 'air spirits, spirits of the wave' and takes a three-part oath of chastity in 'Je le jure.'

clues.clue: Hector Berlioz gave it a glowing review in his final feuilleton; the librettist of The Fair Maid of Perth (another Bizet opera) is Saint-George, who also wrote the libretto for Giselle
examples: In the last feuilleton he ever wrote, Hector Berlioz took no issue with Bizet's inspirations in a glowing review of his opera about two Ceylonese people
The librettist of Giselle, Saint-George, also wrote the libretto to The Fair Maid of Perth, an opera by this composer of Carmen.

clues.clue: In Act I, Nadir sings the aria 'Je crois entendre encore' admitting he betrayed Zurga
examples: Two characters in this opera sing the friendship duet 'Au fond du temple saint', before one of them admits that he had betrayed the other in a solo aria 'Je crois entendre encore'.

works.name: L'Arlésienne Suites
works.description: Two orchestral suites arranged from Bizet's incidental music for Alphonse Daudet's play L'Arlésienne. Both suites feature themes from the Epiphany hymn 'March of the Kings' — in the Suite No. 1's 'Prelude' and Suite No. 2's closing 'Farandole.' The first suite's final movement, 'Carillon,' features horns imitating tolling church bells with a three-note ostinato. The second suite was arranged by Ernest Guiraud. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Written as incidental music for Alphonse Daudet's play L'Arlésienne; arranged into two suites
examples: This French composer of The Pearl Fishers wrote L'Arlesienne. His final opera ends with the title woman being fatally stabbed by Don Jose.
written to accompany an Alphonse Daudet play

clues.clue: The 'March of the Kings' (Epiphany hymn) is quoted in Suite No. 1's 'Prelude' and Suite No. 2's 'Farandole'
examples: This composer quoted the Epiphany hymn 'March of the Kings' throughout his two L'Arlesienne suites.
The prelude to that suite and farandole from a later suite both quote the 'March of the Kings'

clues.clue: Suite No. 1's final movement is 'Carillon,' in which French horns imitate tolling church bells with the three-note ostinato G-sharp, E, F-sharp
examples: The final movement of an orchestral suite by this composer is pervaded by the three-note horn ostinato 'G-sharp, E, F-sharp,' which mimics a carillon.
French horns imitate church bells in the 'Carillon' movement of one suite by this composer

clues.clue: Suite No. 2 was arranged by Ernest Guiraud
examples: The second suite of this name ends with a 'Farandole' and was arranged by Ernest Guiraud.

works.name: Symphony in C
works.description: Written by Bizet at age 17 while a student at the Paris Conservatory, borrowing heavily from Gounod's Symphony in D (his teacher's work). Bizet suppressed it; it was first performed 60 years after his death. The allegretto vivace scherzo opens with a fugato; the adagio second movement also features fugato development. The aria 'De mon amie' was based on its second movement. Inspired Balanchine's ballet The Crystal Palace. Also associated with his Roma Symphony. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Written at age 17; suppressed due to similarity with Gounod's Symphony in D; first performed 60 years after Bizet's death
examples: A youthful symphony by this composer… was first performed 60 years after his death.
This composer was a student of Charles Gounod when he wrote his Symphony in C, which he suppressed due to similarities with his teacher's Symphony in D.

clues.clue: Opens with the tutti melody 'C, down to ascending G-G-C, C-C-E, E-E-G'; the scherzo and adagio both feature fugato writing
examples: A youthful symphony by this composer opens with the tutti melody 'C, down to ascending G-G-C, C-C-E, E-E-G'
The allegretto vivace scherzo of one of this man's symphonies opens with a fugato in which first violins, then second violins, violas, and celli in turn enter with the leggiero theme.

clues.clue: Inspired Balanchine's ballet The Crystal Palace
examples: This piece inspired Balanchine's ballet The Crystal Palace and is scored in the same key as its composer's lifelong pursuit, the 'Roma' Symphony.

works.name: The Fair Maid of Perth and Other Works
works.description: Bizet's other operas include: The Fair Maid of Perth (with libretto by Saint-George, the same person who wrote the libretto for the ballet Giselle), set in Scotland, in which Ralph and Henry Smith duel over Catherine Glover; Don Procopio, which imitates Donizetti's Don Pasquale; and opera fragments. Also wrote four-hands piano miniatures with titles like 'Soap Bubbles' and 'The Spinning Top.' Wikipedia

examples: This composer wrote an opera in which Ralph and Henry Smith duel over the honor of Catherine Glover
The librettist of Giselle, Saint-George, also wrote the libretto to The Fair Maid of Perth, an opera by this composer of Carmen.

examples: This composer's best-known piano music is a set of four-hands miniatures with titles like 'Soap Bubbles' and 'The Spinning Top.'

comprehensive_summary:
Georges Bizet (1838–1875) was a French opera composer best known for Carmen (1875) and The Pearl Fishers (Les pêcheurs de perles, 1863). His teacher at the Paris Conservatory was Charles Gounod. At age 17, Bizet composed a Symphony in C that borrowed heavily from Gounod's Symphony in D — Bizet suppressed it, and it was not performed until 60 years after his death. It later inspired Balanchine's ballet The Crystal Palace.

The Pearl Fishers, set in ancient Sri Lanka (Ceylon), features the famous friendship duet 'Au fond du temple saint' sung by the baritone Zurga and the tenor Nadir, who discover they are both in love with the Brahman priestess Leila. The opera also includes Leila's aria 'Comme autrefois dans la nuit sombre' recalling happier times, and the aria 'Je crois entendre encore' in which Nadir admits betrayal. Hector Berlioz gave The Pearl Fishers a glowing review in his final feuilleton. The librettist of another Bizet opera, The Fair Maid of Perth (in which Ralph and Henry Smith duel over Catherine Glover), was Saint-George — the same person who wrote the libretto for the ballet Giselle.

Bizet wrote incidental music for Alphonse Daudet's play L'Arlésienne, which was arranged into two orchestral suites. Both suites quote the Epiphany hymn 'March of the Kings' — in Suite No. 1's 'Prelude' and Suite No. 2's concluding 'Farandole.' The first suite's final movement, 'Carillon,' features French horns imitating tolling church bells with the three-note ostinato G-sharp, E, F-sharp. Ernest Guiraud (a New Orleans-born French composer) arranged Suite No. 2 and also wrote orchestral recitatives for Carmen and Offenbach's The Tales of Hoffmann.

Carmen (1875), Bizet's final opera, is his masterpiece. The title character sings the Habanera ('L'amour est un oiseau rebelle'), comparing love to a rebellious bird — with a melody Bizet unknowingly borrowed from a Sebastián Iradier song. The soprano character Micaëla, who was not in the Mérimée novella, sings 'Je dis que rien ne m'épouvante' in Act III. The opera ends with Don José stabbing Carmen outside a bullfight arena. Bizet also set words by Charles Grandmougin to music and completed Fromental Halévy's unfinished opera Noé.
